summary: Today’s PaymentsDive recap: 8 new stories, with focus on regulation, product + fintech innovation.

articles.title: Fiserv moves through ‘transition year’
articles.description: During its first-quarter earnings report Tuesday, the payments processor’s CEO cited new executive hires and cost cuts aimed at reviving growth.

articles.title: How Spirit Airlines customers can navigate payments
articles.description: One consumer advocacy group is urging the airline’s customers to seek refunds “quickly” after the carrier’s abrupt shutdown.

articles.title: Amex to sell travel unit stake for $1.5B
articles.description: The card company is divesting its nearly one-third ownership of the corporate travel services firm Global Business Travel Group.

articles.title: State AGs target e-cigarette payments
articles.description: Attorneys general across the U.S. have alerted payments providers that their tobacco and nicotine sales may violate state or federal laws.

articles.title: What keeps SMBs from using embedded payments
articles.description: The window between signup and first transaction is shorter than most platforms think.

articles.title: Nacha confab fun included swag, mixers and games
articles.description: The Smarter Faster Payments conference this week wasn’t all work. There were games, freebies and parties to balance the heady panels.

articles.title: How AI is bettering B2B payments
articles.description: AI is a new tool to help optimize accounts receivable, which has become a “data science challenge” for many large companies, Billtrust’s CEO says.

articles.title: Tourism group decries credit card bill
articles.description: The organization backed by the Electronic Payments Coalition attacked the Consumer Credit Card Competition Act proposal.
